# Chapter 06

[76tcyyvi] The morning after the drive, Flynn’s body still felt like it was moving at eighty even when he stood still. His hands kept remembering the wheel. His ears kept listening for an engine note that wasn’t there. He tried to help Don with the generator and nearly dropped a socket because his fingers wouldn’t stop trembling.

[wg2brrvy] “Adrenaline,” Don said, not unkind. He took the socket from Flynn and set it on the bench like placing a small animal back in its cage. “It’ll leave when it wants.”

[uth5yt85] Cage heard that and laughed from somewhere behind them. “It doesn’t leave,” he said. “It just learns new shapes.”

[hqsadf3j] By late afternoon the heat sat heavy and unmoving. The Compound’s shadows shrank to thin lines under the trailers. Diesel made them pull a tarp over the work area, not for comfort but to keep the metal from burning skin.

[tr0uqx1y] Flynn was under the rat-rod with a wrench, tightening a mount that had shaken loose. Grease streaked his forearms. He liked the dirt. It felt like proof.

[muagzhkm] Birdie crawled beside him and handed him a bolt without being asked. “You look like you belong now,” Birdie said.

[g2lb7vun] Flynn snorted. “I stalled yesterday,” he said.

[xs1fhffq] Birdie shrugged. “Everybody stalls. You didn’t cry.”

[ow10ung3] Flynn thought of the hot prickle behind his eyes and said nothing.

[drad5wva] Diesel called them in before sunset. Not with a shout. With a short whistle that cut clean through the Compound noise. When Diesel whistled, you came.

[pp6nfxvf] They gathered around the fire drum even though the day was still warm. The fire was small, more symbol than heat. Don sat on a crate with his elbows on his knees. Wrench leaned against a trailer tire, arms folded like he was holding himself together. Birdie bounced on his heels, restless.

[taatr9a0] Cage stood instead of sitting. He liked standing when he wanted a room to feel smaller.

[hiuf2zwo] Flynn took the last open spot and tried not to look at Cage too much. The need was there anyway, humming.

[g5rb3atk] Cage held something in his hand. A paper flyer, creased and dirty, like it had been folded and unfolded a hundred times. He didn’t show it yet.

[ixzubvix] “We’ve been living small,” Cage said. “We’ve been careful. We’ve been hiding. That’s not living. That’s waiting.”

[2myxnzoj] Diesel’s face didn’t change. “Waiting keeps us alive,” she said.

[ffpvplca] Cage smiled at her as if she’d said something sweet. “Alive,” he repeated. “Sure.”

[vipzsblg] He lifted the flyer and held it over the fire so the light caught the ink. At the top was a stylized chili pepper in flames. Beneath it, a date and a location printed in block letters.

[nc7wy5hg] Birdie sucked in a breath. “No,” he said, like prayer.

[frrolbq2] Wrench’s jaw tightened.

[65vqzi94] Flynn leaned forward. The words on the flyer were in Spanish and English. The Spanish was bigger.

[hyg5x5ii] **LA CARRERA DEL CHILE ROJO.**

[48icwclj] Under it: *Invitación sólo. Dos millones de dólares.* The purse number was the only language money needed.

[hvacrtqs] Cage watched Flynn watching it. “You know what it is,” he said.

[i7kkrifv] Flynn nodded slowly. He’d heard rumors in the city feeds before his implant had been hard-off, whispered like ghost stories. Illegal races in Mexico that didn’t just go fast, they went *real*. People died. People came back. People kept racing anyway.

[ym8tdgb1] “It’s real?” Flynn asked.

[oojarwqo] Birdie laughed without humor. “It’s the realest thing,” he said. “It’s the race. The one everybody pretends isn’t happening.”

[ml4rvftl] Don finally spoke. “Castellanos runs it,” he said.

[bvphlm6z] Cage’s eyes slid to Don. “Don Castellanos,” Cage said, savoring the full name like it was a brand.

[kown5fl9] Don’s mouth twitched. “Not me,” he said. “The other one.”

[ptkmnpi5] Diesel looked at Don sharply. There was history there Flynn didn’t have.

[wbvfbk7r] Cage tapped the flyer. “Two million,” he said. “Plus the trophy car. A ’71 Hemi ’Cuda. Winner takes it. Losers take what the desert leaves them.”

[1hfrigfm] Birdie’s eyes went wide. “A ’Cuda,” he whispered.

[kaq8aynu] Wrench’s voice came out low. “We’re not invited.”

[n15wqhk6] Cage nodded. “Not yet.”

[ojztj9al] He let that hang, the way a preacher let silence do work.

[g1cdkh5l] “We get invited,” Cage said, “when we show up with a car that matters.”

[2k6ydpx5] Diesel’s gaze narrowed. “And where do we get that,” she asked, already hearing the answer.

[vz8cfyir] Cage looked past her at the rat-rod, at the patchwork cars, at the salvaged engines. “We don’t,” he said. “Not out of the boneyard. Not out of scrap.”

[2qqfpwsv] He lowered the flyer and brought up a second thing from his pocket: a printed image on cheap paper, grainy and slightly blurred. A car behind glass.

[ypen4dqy] Even in the bad print, Flynn recognized the shape. Long hood. Fastback. The stance of something built to outrun consequences.

[xp1ymgms] “Shelby,” Birdie breathed.

[90tjkla1] Cage nodded once. “’67 GT500,” he said. “Original. Real. Sitting in Phoenix in a private collection like a caged animal.”

[xaizai1f] Flynn felt his skin prickle. Phoenix was close. Phoenix was watched.

[os6xbo2w] Diesel’s face went still. “That’s not a find,” she said. “That’s a theft.”

[j4qyw1wd] Cage’s smile returned. “It’s a liberation,” he said. “Those cars aren’t meant to sit under glass while pod people take pictures. They’re meant to run.”

[cwedrch9] Wrench pushed off the tire. “That’s a billionaire’s garage,” he said. “Security. Cameras. Private guards. You’re talking about walking into a museum with a crowbar.”

[cyjtalo0] “Museums are for the dead,” Cage said. “We’re alive.”

[r7fove9u] Diesel rubbed her thumb across a grease line on her knuckle as if trying to erase it. “We’ve never done something like that,” she said. “We steal parts. We don’t steal whole cars from people who can buy the police.”

[oxn2emqf] Cage turned to Flynn. “You hear her,” he said. “That’s fear talking.”

[imhhg5lm] Flynn’s mouth opened and nothing came out. Diesel wasn’t afraid of engines. Diesel wasn’t afraid of work. If Diesel was afraid, it meant something.

[eclg9mvh] Birdie jumped in, voice too bright. “We could do it,” he said. “We can do anything if we do it fast.”

[ox7i6g18] Don frowned. “Fast isn’t a plan,” he said.

[6aflkn4q] Cage waved a hand as if brushing away a fly. “It’s not complicated,” he said. “We go at night. We take three people. We’re in and out.”

[fvzmwabs] Diesel’s laugh was short and sharp. “That’s what everyone says before it goes wrong,” she said.

[0fyg9b1r] Cage stepped closer to the fire and the light cut his face into hard planes. “Listen,” he said, and the word carried weight because Cage didn’t often ask.

[iqchhpb7] “That car belonged to a man’s father,” Cage said. He pointed at Don without looking at him. “A driver. A real one. The kind we tell stories about. The kind the state erased and called it progress.”

[ay051cwa] Don’s shoulders tightened. He stared into the fire.

[bahpu8yg] Cage kept going. “If we bring it back, we don’t just get invited. We honor what they tried to bury. We take something they stole and we make it loud again.”

[3cemr0co] Birdie nodded hard, already converted.

[b1jthhzu] Wrench’s eyes went to Diesel, then away. He didn’t look like a man arguing. He looked like a man weighing whether to leave.

[w0sniwv3] Diesel held Cage’s gaze. “And the man who owns it,” she said. “What do we do about him.”

[fzx88g4v] Cage shrugged. “We don’t hurt him,” he said. “We take the car. He wakes up in the morning and calls his insurance and cries into a pillow. That’s it.”

[62ymvpcu] Wrench made a sound like disbelief. “You don’t get a Shelby out of a guarded collection without somebody bleeding,” he said.

[or3ziuco] Cage’s eyes glittered. “Then we make sure it’s not us,” he said.

[2chqlcwu] Flynn felt something cold slide down his spine. Cage said it like a joke, like a clever line. Flynn heard the shape of violence under it.

[q0jtxbhb] Diesel spoke carefully, like she was walking around a mine. “Phoenix isn’t the desert,” she said. “It’s cameras and drones and cops who get paid to care.”

[hpkd1hc5] “Which is why we need you,” Cage said. He pointed at Flynn now. “You’re the tech kid. You grew up inside their systems. You know how the eyes work.”

[xrimelfo] Flynn blinked. He hadn’t realized Cage had already decided his role.

[evszimfg] “I don’t—” Flynn started.

[ukqrcyde] Cage cut him off with a small smile. “You do,” he said. “You just don’t know you do yet.”

[nxvckcnj] Birdie leaned in, excited. “Like hacking,” he said.

[puahtfvp] Diesel’s eyes narrowed at Birdie. “Not like hacking,” she said. “Like surviving.”

[1hrcbxut] Cage lifted the flyer again. The chili pepper seemed to dance in the firelight. “You want to keep making fuel and fixing mounts forever,” he said. “Or you want to go to Mexico and race the best outlaws on the continent for two million dollars.”

[imzrdesk] Flynn felt his heart pound. Two million was a number from another universe. It sounded like freedom. It sounded like a trap.

[pfjl4p5r] Wrench spoke up, voice rough. “We’re talking about stealing from a rich man,” he said. “That’s not the same as scavenging. That’s a line.”

[9jtwn5wi] Cage looked at Wrench for the first time like he was noticing him. “Lines are for roads,” he said.

[a0c7ja95] Wrench’s face tightened. “You know what I mean.”

[v2rmieot] Cage nodded slowly. “I know,” he said. “And I’m telling you the line is a lie they drew to keep you small.”

[zeiuwfqs] Diesel exhaled through her nose. “You’re good at words,” she said.

[nbcbbivj] Cage smiled. “Words are cheap,” he said. “Cars aren’t.”

[ckmb9hiy] Don leaned forward. “If it’s my father’s car,” he said, careful, “then why hasn’t it been crushed like the others.”

[t2rdoovx] Diesel’s eyes flicked to him. She didn’t answer. She was thinking the same thing.

[f05bgi8b] Cage spread his hands. “Because rich men keep trophies,” he said. “Because the ban is for the rest of us. Because they like looking at what they took.”

[1ol5s58f] The fire popped and a spark rose and died. Flynn watched it and thought of his own city apartment, the quiet hum of climate control, the soft voice of his implant telling him he was fine. He realized he’d never been fine.

[qlnbzn0w] Diesel stood. She didn’t announce it. She simply stood, and that changed the air.

[3d4kbpwn] “If you do this,” Diesel said, “you do it with a plan. A real plan. Not a story.” She looked at Cage. “And you don’t bring Birdie.”

[6uokvcaj] Birdie’s mouth fell open. “What?”

[ipc4rhzs] “No,” Diesel said, without softness. “You’re fifteen. You’re a liability.”

[dr0lvdje] Birdie looked at Cage for rescue.

[wvbij2bb] Cage didn’t give it. He watched Diesel like he was watching a door he wanted open. “Fine,” he said. “No Birdie. You happy.”

[8wzzoslf] Birdie’s face flushed with humiliation.

[dvum02xe] Wrench’s shoulders loosened a fraction, like a rope had been cut.

[fnhj1c21] Cage turned to Flynn again. “You come,” he said. “Diesel comes. I come. Don stays here with Wrench. Someone has to keep the Compound alive.”

[mlynr3s1] Don started to protest, then didn’t. His loyalty was not to Cage. His loyalty was to the engines.

[8ksmgrx6] Diesel rubbed her forehead. “If we pull a job in Phoenix, the heat follows us back,” she said. “You understand that.”

[eycc5kkr] Cage nodded. “Then we leave,” he said. “We get the Shelby. We go to Mexico. We don’t sit here and wait for the state to choke us slowly.”

[yixnskka] Flynn felt his stomach drop. Leave. Mexico. It was happening fast, like the car surging when the clutch caught.

[7khiqlvn] Wrench spoke softly. “You’re escalating,” he said.

[xsexg6an] Cage looked at him, amused. “Escalation is just momentum,” he said.

[bcsqu2bb] Diesel’s eyes flashed. “Momentum kills,” she said.

[bnf3kj8w] Cage’s smile stayed. “Only if you let go of the wheel,” he said.

[aqgmygua] Flynn realized Cage was looking at him when he said it, as if this was a lesson too.

[kedwejal] Diesel’s gaze moved to Flynn. “You can say no,” she said.

[2benami1] The words hit Flynn like cold water. In the city no one had ever said he could say no. They’d just guided him around his own refusal until it became compliance.

[vocz6hbq] Cage’s face didn’t change, but the air around him tightened.

[9q7tzmpt] Flynn looked at the flyer again. The chili pepper. The purse. The date. Mexico like a mouth waiting to close.

[ux2rtyjv] He thought of the wash, the moment the car slid and he corrected, the laugh that had come out of him like a confession. He thought of his life before that, the quiet deadness.

[hhlin62e] “I’m in,” Flynn said.

[y3mrzusl] Cage’s smile sharpened, pleased.

[j0erk5yp] Diesel closed her eyes for a second, then opened them. “Then we plan,” she said.

[muiqnwij] Cage finally sat down, as if the decision had been a weight he’d been holding. “Good,” he said. “Tonight we talk timing. Tomorrow we start watching Phoenix.”

[qun4j7mx] Wrench stared at the fire. His face looked older in the flicker. “This is how it goes wrong,” he said, so quiet Flynn wasn’t sure Cage heard.

[5o93z0ey] Cage did hear. He leaned forward, voice gentle. “It only goes wrong if you don’t believe,” he said.

[m8iya0gi] Wrench flinched like Cage had touched him.

[eyjcgtoq] Don stood and tossed his coffee into the sand. “I’m going back to the engine,” he said. He walked away because engines were simpler than men.

[h2kmyzrs] Birdie stayed by the fire, jaw tight, humiliated and hungry. Flynn could feel the kid’s anger like heat.

[mpmq2tsc] Diesel pulled a notebook from her pocket. Paper, not a screen. She wrote while Cage talked, making lists: tools, time windows, routes, names.

[uo2quz7t] Flynn watched her handwriting and felt something shift. This wasn’t just a story around a fire. This was a project with consequences.

[rr16pqc2] Cage’s voice rose as he painted the heist like it was already done. “We go in quiet,” he said. “We don’t touch what we don’t need. We take the Shelby and we leave. Like ghosts.”

[kuxpq3at] Diesel didn’t look up. “Ghosts bleed,” she said.

[w9rbtfxw] Cage laughed. “Not if they’re fast,” he said.

[uc3d0qjt] Flynn stared into the fire and imagined the Shelby behind glass. He imagined touching the steering wheel, the thin worn rim that had held a man’s father in place while he outran the world.

[qoqsmezm] He imagined cameras, alarms, guards with guns. He imagined the sound of a real city at night, not pods humming but people yelling, sirens, panic.

[vbquhphv] In the city, danger had always been abstract. Here it was forming a shape with a date on it.

[btsgb9pn] Cage slapped Flynn’s shoulder hard enough to jolt him. “You’re going to be useful,” Cage said.

[ryvrtopg] Flynn nodded because he didn’t know what else to do.

[lhzt1hqs] Diesel’s pen scratched across paper. Outside the circle of firelight the desert waited, patient as ever.

[ttsvuwzq] When the meeting broke, the Compound didn’t feel like home anymore. It felt like a staging area.

[0pmswpkz] Flynn lay in his trailer later, listening to the generator and the distant highway hush. He couldn’t sleep. His mind kept returning to Phoenix, to the glass, to Cage’s smile.

[jlpzkzko] His implant was still hard-off, but he could almost hear the city anyway: safety warnings, sponsored calm, the soft promise that nothing would happen.

[dpwfzfv5] He realized he didn’t want that promise. Not anymore.

[pge4o66n] Outside, someone revved an engine once, just to hear it. The sound died into the night like a challenge.

[p9zerpej] Flynn stared at the trailer ceiling and thought: this is the point of no return. And in the dark, with his heart still racing, he found he was smiling.
